title: Wolfspeed's quarterly margins and cash burn improved despite falling revenue
text: Wolfspeed reported fiscal Q3 2026 revenue of $150.2 million, representing a 10.6% decline from the previous quarter and a 19% decrease year-over-year. The company's Materials Products division experienced significant contraction, falling 36% annually to $50.1 million due to weakened demand and competitive pressures. Power Products revenue reached $100.1 million, down 15.4% sequentially but relatively stable year-over-year. A notable development was the shift of all device production to 200mm wafers at the Mohawk Valley facility, with the Durham fab's 150mm production concluding ahead of schedule in November. The company remains focused on AI data-center applications, which expanded approximately 30% sequentially during the quarter. Despite revenue challenges, operational metrics improved substantially. Non-GAAP gross margin recovered to -20.6% from -34% the prior quarter, while operating expenses dropped to $61 million from $108 million annually. The company reduced operating cash burn to -$83.8 million from -$142.1 million year-over-year, and capital expenditures declined sharply to $5 million after netting $33 million in New York State incentives. Wolfspeed strengthened its financial position by raising $476 million through convertible notes and equity offerings, reducing first-lien debt by approximately 43% and lowering projected annual interest expenses by around €62 million. The company maintains $1.2 billion in liquidity heading into Q4. For the upcoming quarter, management projects revenues between $140-160 million while emphasizing that improving factory utilization remains one of the most important levers for margin expansion.
